answer:
**The poem is "Tanhai" (Solitude), Chapter 25 of *Bang-E-Dara Part 2* by Allama Iqbal.**[^1]

It consoles a solitary heart by declaring the stars as companions ("Are the stars sorrowful in the night? / Are they not your companions!") and portraying Nature as its kindred spirit ("For Nature is your kindred spirit, O heart!"). The specified couplet appears in the fourth stanza:

> These lovely pearls, so bright they are,  
> Are but your tears, become a star.[^2]

[^1]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, Ch. 25 "Tanhai" (/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/25-tanhai/translation/).

[^2]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, Ch. 25 "Tanhai" (/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/25-tanhai/translation/#p-36f218).

args.query: These lovely pearls, so bright they are
